title: Add user search endpoint — SQL injection vulnerability
author: backend-intern
pr_description: Implements a search endpoint that allows finding users by username pattern. Uses direct SQL for performance.
diff:
diff --git a/db.py b/db.py
index aaa1111..bbb2222 100644
--- a/db.py
+++ b/db.py
@@ -18,6 +18,16 @@ def get_user(session, user_id: int):
     return session.query(User).filter(User.id == user_id).first()
 
 
+def search_users(session, username: str):
+    """Search users by username pattern."""
+    cursor = session.connection().connection.cursor()
+    query = f"SELECT id, username, email FROM users WHERE username LIKE '%{username}%'"
+    cursor.execute(query)
+    rows = cursor.fetchall()
+    return [
+        {"id": r[0], "username": r[1], "email": r[2]}
+        for r in rows
+    ]
 
 
 def create_user(session, username: str, email: str):

gold_issues.description: SQL injection vulnerability: user input is directly interpolated into SQL query using f-string. Attacker can inject arbitrary SQL via the username parameter.
